name: Amy Klobuchar
subject: Thank You, Minnesota!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Klobuchar for Minnesota
body:
Hi,
Thank you, Minnesota! I am honored and humbled to continue serving as your United States Senator.
---
I'm also pleased that my colleague Tina Smith won her race, and that Tim Walz and Peggy Flanagan will be in the Governor's office!
The ballot was more than just a ballot this election. Big stakes, big choices, and bigger ideals.
Minnesotans voted for our dreams, not our fears.
We voted for common sense, not blistering words.
We voted for getting things done, not gamesmanship.
We voted for what politics can be -- should be -- and with your help, will be. We voted for all of that. And we won.
There are so many who have stood by me in this fight. This would not have been possible without you. Every phone call made and door knocked, every one of you made a difference.
**Thank you.**
Now, the work begins.
We have to stand up for everyone. So tonight, I'm talking to all of you. For those who voted for us. For those who didn't. For those who stayed home, tired of the divisiveness in our politics.
Because whether you're a Democrat or Republican or an Independent, we are all Minnesotans. We are all Americans.
And the America I believe in, the America I'm optimistic about, is an America that does right by its people. An America that's as good as its promise. That's what we're fighting for.
You are the basis of not just our victory tonight, but our victories to come. You are the reason I'm optimistic about the future of Minnesota and our country.
Sincerely,
Amy Klobuchar

Friend:

I can't thank you enough. We now have our moment to carve out our mark in history as One Minnesota.

We will work together to make sure that every Minnesotan who wants a good job can find a good job; deal with health care costs; tackle climate change; and keep the promise of a great education to every Minnesota child.

What many describe as the Minnesota Miracle – that’s just what we do here. Let’s double down on the next Minnesota Miracle – inspiring our kids to dream, setting lofty goals and lifting each other up to accomplish them. We don’t fear the future, we create the future.

To our incredible volunteers and supporters – all over the state of Minnesota people came out to help us, to make calls, to knock doors, to post on social media, to talk to their friends and families about our campaign. We are so, so grateful.

We are One Minnesota – let’s take our state into the future together.

Thank you,

Tim

| | Peter, When you love this country, you fight for the people who make it work. **And tonight, the hardworking women and men of Ohio voted to continue our fight together.** Ohio showed the country that progressives can win -- and win decisively -- in the heartland. Tonight, Ohio showed the country that by putting people first and by honoring the dignity of work, we can carry a state Donald Trump won by _nearly 10 points._ And we showed that we do it without compromising on women's rights or civil rights or LGBTQ rights. We do it without caving to Wall Street or the drug companies or the gun lobby. As progressives, we know a number of things. We know how to get things done, to bend the arc of history towards justice. As progressives, we know you build the economy from the middle out, by putting people first, by rewarding their work, not by cutting taxes for the rich. **And as we celebrate the dignity of work, we unify, we do not divide.** We do not appeal to some by pushing down others. We do not lie. We do not engage in hate speech. And we do not rip babies from their families at the border. This is our America: We will never give up the hallowed ground of patriotism to the extremists. I repeat: We will never ever give up the hallowed ground of patriotism to the extremists at the Statehouse and in the White House. **We love this country. We fight for the people who make it work. And we will -- thanks to all of you -- continue that fight for another six years.** Let our country -- our nation's citizens, our Democratic Party, my fellow elected officials all over the country -- let them all cast their eyes towards the heartland, to the industrial Midwest, to OUR Great Lakes state. Let them hear what we say. Let them see what we do. And we will show America how we celebrate Organized Labor and all workers, the waitress in Dayton, the office worker in Toledo, the nurse in Columbus, the mineworker in Coshocton. _That is the message coming out of Ohio in 2018_ \-- and that is the blueprint for our nation in 2020. Onward, Sherrod | | | [Contribute](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffd5d/4462dc26/743c7b2d/201f0399/1418999891/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sbe181106pm3oh?refcode=sbe181106pm3oh_m")

name: Bob Menendez
subject: You did this
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Menendez for Senate
body:
Friend,

Because of your hard work and dedication to this fight, I am proud to be able to say:

**We won.**

I cannot begin to explain my gratitude for all you have done to make this victory possible. Your support for our team has been incredible. Because of you, friend, we are a step closer to putting a real check on Donald Trump's reckless agenda.

We are a step closer to stopping the attacks on affordable health care, protecting Medicare, Medicaid, and Social Security, and we are a step closer to ensuring that New Jersey families have the support they need to keep moving forward.

This is not the end of the journey, it is the beginning. We have a lot of work ahead of us, but I wanted to take tonight to tell you how incredibly thankful I am to have you in my corner. Tomorrow we get back to work.

Thank you from the bottom of my heart,

Bob

name: Tina Smith
subject: Thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
Friend, I am so grateful tonight.

| [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4d2f3/c1f7015/6e0b2984/1140d54c/1419707219/VEsH/ "https://tinaforminnesota.com") Friend, I am so grateful tonight to everyone in Minnesota who put their faith in me. I promise I will work hard every day on your behalf as your United States Senator. But, as we've said all along, this election wasn't about me, a political party, or even a President -- it's about the people of Minnesota. This campaign was for the mother of two with a pre-existing condition who needs health care, the grandparents on a fixed income who can't afford the high cost of prescription drugs, the worker who needs to make a living wage so they can put food on the table and a roof over their head, the student struggling with the crushing load of debt, and for everyone in Minnesota that needs a government that works for them -- not just those who can write big checks. Tonight everyone on this team sent a message that when we work together a campaign for all of us can win -- and we can do it the right way. We knocked on hundreds of thousands of doors, made hundreds of thousands of phone calls, and raised the funds to build this campaign one small donation at a time. I can't ever thank you enough for everything you did to build this campaign, and for the sacrifices you made to do it -- but if I can make one more ask before I say goodnight to you, it would be this: Don't stop. This victory tonight was made by your hard work, but if we let up, even for a second, we can't make any of the progress we all are fighting for. It's going to take this same effort from people across the country to change what happens in Washington. So tonight celebrate, hug your loved ones, and ring in the night with your friends. But tomorrow, we all must get right back to work. Thank you again for everything you did to make this possible. I can't ever thank you enough. Tina
---

name: Martin Heinrich
subject: Thank you for believing in me
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MARTIN HEINRICH FOR SENATE
body:
|
---

| | | We did it.

Tonight, after a long campaign and a lot of hard work, we moved New Mexico forward.

**I just stepped off stage from giving my victory speech and wanted to thank you personally for reading all these emails, supporting my campaign, and returning me to the U.S. Senate.**

Over my first term in the Senate, I'm proud to have focused on delivering results for New Mexico instead of scoring political points on the cable news shows. As an engineer, solving problems is in my DNA -- playing politics is not.

But I've found that despite all the chaos and heated rhetoric in Washington, you can still find ways to make a real difference for New Mexico.

That's why I look forward to continuing to deliver investments and forward-looking missions for New Mexico's bases and national labs. And we can create thousands of new jobs in growing fields as New Mexico continues to make outsized contributions to national security and scientific innovation. We can work to protect our public lands and continue to expand health care coverage, strengthen education, and grow wages.

None of this would be possible without you. You stood with me, supported me, contributed to my campaign, volunteered, and most importantly, voted. This is not just my victory. It is yours, and it is New Mexico's.

Results are still rolling in. We won't know until tomorrow, or maybe even later, what the political landscape will be. But I know that I will still be working for New Mexico and working to make you proud.

Thank you for supporting my campaign and believing in my vision for this state.

Sincerely,

Martin

name: Tim Ryan
subject: I'm going back to Congress to fight for you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tim Ryan for Congress
body:
As the rest of the results come in, one thing is abundantly clear: Americans want change. | |
---
Friends, **I am grateful to the citizens of Ohio's 13th Congressional District for placing their faith in me to represent them for another term in the House of Representatives! ** I would also like to congratulate all those who stepped up to run for office -- and, of course, thank all of you who went to the polls and made your voice heard. As the rest of the results come in, one thing is abundantly clear: **Americans want change.** We've had enough of the division, hate, and fear-mongering of the last two years -- that's why we've elected a new generation of leaders Congress to change our country's course. Democrats will fight to improve our healthcare system, bring high wage jobs into communities that need them, make critical investments in infrastructure and education, and provide the much-needed oversight of an Administration riddled with unprecedented levels of corruption. Today -- from Ohio, to the Deep South, to the West Coast, and everywhere in between -- working people have sent a clear message: The billionaires-first, workers-last agenda of this Republican Congress is over. **It's time for an agenda that works for working people.** This has been a good night for the United States of America. Thanks for everything you do, \-- Congressman Tim Ryan [DONATE](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a9082/5486deaa/246ee621/dcf6c1b/2138190533/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tr_nov18?refcode=em20181106_reelection")

name: Tim Kaine
subject: Thank you, Derek
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
| | Wow, Derek.

I can't thank you enough for all you've given over the past year -- not to mention the years that have come before this one. You've been with me for a long time, and I am humbled by your continued encouragement and support.

It's been a long few months on the campaign trail, but tonight I find myself more energized than ever -- honored to return to the Senate for another term and get back to work opening more doors of opportunity for every Virginian and American. I'm energized not just by the work ahead, but by knowing that I'll be doing it with some incredible colleagues at my side. My friends Bobby Scott, Don McEachin, Don Beyer, and Gerry Connolly will be returning to the halls of the Capitol. And I am so gratified to be able to say tonight that they won't be alone -- they'll be bringing with them some of the smartest, strongest leaders I've seen in a long while: **Elaine Luria, Abigail Spanberger, and Jennifer Wexton.**

These new leaders won't just serve as strong progressive voices in Congress -- though they will do that, and do it well. They are also ushering in a tide of women in leadership that is both overdue and hard-won. My work has long been made possible -- and made better -- by the work of strong women in my life and in my office, ahead of me and by my side. I'm grateful for that, and I am so glad for the people of Virginia that they have these three women leading them in the House of Representatives.

We have made incredible strides forward tonight in this Commonwealth. And not just through our victories. It was an honor to serve on the Democratic ticket with Vangie Williams, Leslie Cockburn, Jennifer Lewis, and Anthony Flaccavento. Their races were tough -- and though the final tally wasn't what we'd hoped for, their successes have shown us that Virginians are ready for dynamic, thoughtful leadership. Their inroads have shown us that every county and city in Virginia can be competitive, and must be engaged. Democrats are not only Northern Virginia; Democrats are not only Richmond. We are the Star City. We are Danville. We are Gloucester. We are Abingdon.

I look forward to continuing to work with Vangie, Leslie, Jennifer, and Anthony in their communities, and I know that their hard work this year will help our Commonwealth continue to grow.
* * *
**Virginia has shown America the way -- and set a model for our country to follow.** We flipped more than 25% of our House seats. We did it with three strong women. We hadn't won the 2nd district in years -- we hadn't won the 7th or the 10th district in decades. We now have a 7-4 Democratic majority -- which we haven't had for decades! **All around this country, people are coming together to make a statement about who America is, and who America isn't. We are a kind-hearted, inclusive, optimistic nation. Over the past months and years, that optimism led to a surge of activism in countless communities across all 50 states. And tonight, that optimistic activism has paid off right here in Virginia! I'm deeply grateful for everything you've done to get us here.**

Thank you. When Virginia votes, Virginia wins. When Virginia wins, Virginia leads. And when Virginia leads, America wins! Tonight, we'll celebrate how Virginia paved the way for the rest of our country -- and tomorrow, we'll get right to work putting our country back on the right track.

_-Tim_
---

subject: Peter -- thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Committee to Re-Elect Linda Sanchez
body:
Peter, election results are still coming in nationwide, but I'm pleased to announce that I've been re-elected to serve California's 38th District in Congress!

So I just want to take a moment to say: **thank you.** Your continued trust and support is something that I never take for granted.

As I've said time and again, this year's election was about so much more than any one candidate or which party makes the most gains tonight -- it was about what kind of future we want for our children and country.

We now have the opportunity to bring about the change we so desperately need.

There's a lot of work to do in the days, months, and years ahead. But I promise you this: I'll always fight for working families like yours.

More than ever, I'm fired up. I can't wait to get back to work. And I'm so glad to know you're with me.

-Linda

name: Ben Mitchell
subject: FW: Get Out And Vote
body:
We’re on our way to the victory night party, but I wanted to make sure you saw Mike’s email that he sent earlier.

Polls are still open, turnout is strong, but we need to make sure everyone does their part!

Grab your friends and family now and head on to the polls before they close. You can verify your voter registration and lookup your voting precinct at vote.gop.

Ben Mitchell
Campaign Manager

---Begin Forwarded Message---

Friend,

Today's the day! We have all been working so hard to make it to Election Day and I wanted to say thank you.

Polls are still open, and Hoosiers are coming out strong to send a message to the DC Swamp that they’re ready to retire Sleepin’ Joe.

Be sure you call and text your friends to remind them and then get out and vote to support President Trump’s America-First Agenda.

I’ll see you at the victory night party.

Let’s win this!

Mike

name: Stacey Abrams
subject: This race isn't over.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stacey Abrams for Governor
body:
We will continue to work hard until the very end. We will fight for every vote, just like I will fight for you as Governor. |

| | | Friend, When you chose me as your Democratic nominee, I made you a vow: In our Georgia, no one will be unseen, unheard, or uninspired. We know fulfilling that vow will take effort. It takes commitment to the whole truth. It takes hard work. But as I told you then, hard work is in our bones - and we have proven that every day on this campaign. We will continue to work hard until the very end. We will fight for every vote, just like I will fight for you as Governor. And that means keeping our eyes on the process to ensure that this election is conducted fairly. If you voted using a provisional ballot, or if you had any trouble voting today, I need you to call our **Voter Protection Hotline at 1-888-730-5816** to confirm that your vote was counted. **[And I need you to make a donation to support our campaign through this final stretch. We have some of the best legal minds in the country ready to make sure every vote is counted, but we need your help.](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa7cb/34a064cc/22cc1f1/70cd448/2360570765/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUxMTA2MjAxOC40LWZ1bGwifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sya-november?refcode=IE11062018.4-full")** In Georgia, civil rights has always been an act of will and a battle for our souls. And because we have been fighting this fight since our beginnings, we have learned a fundamental truth: Democracy only works when we work for it. Tonight, we have closed the gap between yesterday and tomorrow. We don't know what tomorrow will bring, but I know I can count on you to see this campaign through to the end. We're in this fight together, Stacey Abrams

Peter,

Many of you have seen the results. For the first time since the Texas 10th was gerrymandered fifteen years ago, last night the District was in play. Before this election, McCaul's margin of victory had been 63% (2004), 14.9% (2006), 10.8% (2008), 31.7% (2010), 24.2% (2012), 28.1% (2014), and 18.9% (2016). Coming within 4% this year represents a huge change in this District, for the better. All of this progress is owed to the thousands of people who supported this campaign, in ways large and small. Almost none of you knew me before last year, and yet you worked to elect me to Congress. You gave your time and your money, you gave your hearts and minds, you believed and took action. Our work together, our sacrifices and commitment, was for the people of this District. We know this work was not in vain. From the City of Austin to Austin County, from Brenham to Bastrop, in each of the communities of this District, we made an impact. We helped build a foundation. We inspired hope and a renewed commitment to organizing for positive social change. I hope to speak to many of you in the days and weeks ahead, to better express my thanks and appreciation, and to learn more about what needs to be done in the Texas 10th. For now, please know that my heart is full, thanks to you. With love and respect,
Mike

clean_body: = Join our team! = or =. Peter, Many of you have seen the results. For the first time since the Texas 10th was gerrymandered fifteen years ago, last night the District was in play. Before this election, McCaul's margin of victory had been 63% (2004), 14.9% (2006), 10.8% (2008), 31.7% (2010), 24.2% (2012), 28.1% (2014), and 18.9% (2016). Coming within 4% this year represents a huge change in this District, for the better. All of this progress is owed to the thousands of people who supported this campaign, in ways large and small. Almost none of you knew me before last year, and yet you worked to elect me to Congress. You gave your time and your money, you gave your hearts and minds, you believed and took action. Our work together, our sacrifices and commitment, was for the people of this District. We know this work was not in vain. From the City of Austin to Austin County, from Brenham to Bastrop, in each of the communities of this District, we made an impact. We helped build a foundation. We inspired hope and a renewed commitment to organizing for positive social change. I hope to speak to many of you in the days and weeks ahead, to better express my thanks and appreciation, and to learn more about what needs to be done in the Texas 10th. For now, please know that my heart is full, thanks to you. With love and respect, Mike = = = = = Paid for by Mike Siegel for Congress: PO Box 2409, Austin, TX 78701.

body:
| | Think. Pray. Act. at the Intersection of Faith & Politics.
---
[ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iTtMewf2DL0D-tj3igeTtq5wiFTJ84rJRz6pz-J4jf1EfsMLlZUBHpGemfbAFZDqDQ&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181031intersect01&n=1)
| Intersect – Special Edition! The Intersect is a weekly email where we **PRAY** , **THINK** , and **ACT** at the intersection of faith and politics. If you are not receiving Intersect every Wednesday and would like to, **[subscribe here](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iTtMewf2DL0D-tj3igeTtq5wiFTJ84rJRz6pz-J4jf1E7kOvKDs6QUYHK1iWazVowA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181031intersect01&n=2)**. **In this week's Intersect, read about:**
* _Democrats Retake Control of House, Republicans Hold Senate Majority_
* _Pakistan Government Stops Asia Bibi From Fleeing Country_
* _Trainee Kills Utah Mayor, Guard Member in Afghanistan_
* _One in Three College Students Believe Physical Violence is Justified to Prevent ‘Hate Speech’_

* * *
# Democrats Retake Control of House, Republicans Hold Senate Majority
Democrats reclaimed control of the House of Representatives Tuesday night after eight years out of power dealing a major setback to President Trump's legislative agenda -- but Republicans were able to add to their majority in the Senate, and with it, the ability to confirm crucial judicial nominees. The split decision on Capitol Hill follows one of the most intense and chaotic midterm campaign seasons in recent memory, in which President Trump barnstormed the country for GOP candidates, and powerful Democrats - including predecessor Barack Obama - did the same for the other side. For his part, Trump was able to help prevent a total Democratic takeover in Congress. He reacted positively but tersely to the results early Wednesday, writing on Twitter, "Tremendous success tonight. Thank you to all!" _[read more](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=KVYzWTFUuJ0SsyVZLAg-7KhgymeBWCJ5z6VS6DGe2fKGNsYlmlHOFhfJKPqeRIRwsRwofjq1vMbfwxMIEJB3U4dgkY0Ybn01lziAjexjV_bgVQCUQ1pjsi6AQWe2QB6DwRAiRsHrb2xsozv5xH6NX-_lj1fwvnGdRI507oWrQik&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181031intersect01&n=3)_ **THINK.**
There were significant victories for Republicans in the Senate. Senator Ted Cruz retained his Senate Seat against Beto O’Rourke in Texas. Republican Josh Hawley defeated Senator Claire McCaskill in Missouri, Republican Mike Braun defeated Democratic incumbent Joe Donnelly in Indiana and Republican Kevin Cramer defeated Senator Heidi Heitkamp in North Dakota. It’s interesting to note that Democratic [Senator Joe Manchin won his Senate race](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=GjwzQmjoNJNKZ7-eUAZR_CG3wJbQir4aa4SlMdbQUN098AbOH_oNCZ7x0WC7oM6hRTqC8jdxcOEZ6VZJ1U6M8pK8yrNaQNJiP9VDB1y3Bjrq3mhvJECeBvxVVjkz3CdnoIbw_WYhn_zBeP9sA9TEdtgwRE9UHDp7ya0jJWJvqNWuIsgmSulg0qJdfX30cgvme6cUi8AgTnvsIu1zF-kyBg&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181031intersect01&n=4), and he was the only Democrat to vote in support of confirming Kavanaugh to the Supreme Court. Important issues like immigration reform will now likely be stuck in a gridlock between a Republican-held Senate and a Democratic-held House. This will cause many people to look ahead to the 2020 elections where one party will need to gain control of Congress to advance policy measures. Needless to say, in order to avoid gridlock over the next two years, both sides will need to work together. In important governor races, Republican [Ron DeSantis defeated Democrat Andrew Gillum in Florida](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=oGkNRIpNU-65tzHrfmdwr4gBY4wDqqaPDF1WKQtzI6SeXV30xnneHMg66rT9r0ifXlZKdo7bdsFo6aTu7nPgen6lLAm6ryzDlSOnq_8oDTV8w9TsFAPuY5whIF3Ihe4e7KYo_fusDKEkY8bSoHEimg&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181031intersect01&n=5) by just one percentage point. In the [Kansas Governor race](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=KVYzWTFUuJ0SsyVZLAg-7KhgymeBWCJ5z6VS6DGe2fI9qDQlcrK0RAoMZwWlypqfxFuNK-m0XgF6G3gey4vCDVlZ1iGo1R43FDjXYEmg36uCU1ROiGfsqrBZNkNNrtBDIclSWUS4IOhXwJy88hgr1lOkzWRn9m4309w4fbHgwwI&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181031intersect01&n=6), Democratic State Senator Laura Kelly beat Republican Secretary of State Kris Kobach. And Colorado elected the nation’s first openly gay governor. With so many elections, the results are simply too many to list here. But we want to thank you for faithfully praying for the elections, voting, and helping others get ready to vote! These elections were important from the local level all the way to the federal level. **PRAY.**
We want to encourage you to pray for your newly elected leaders. Look up their names, write them down, and pray for them even before they take the oath of office (1 Timothy 2:1-4). **ACT.**
Take a few minutes to read My Faith Vote’s article by Jason Yates, “[God Is Not Surprised by the Election Results](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iZwN3saXAy-Y-UHwwC213Xlnn4bVFc-6IerhL0u1MQRCaHu99Dy2z7aKMEVT0HgHJLotWYSLvnj2G6pwgxnLlCU&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181031intersect01&n=7).” And then, with your family or with a church group, discuss an action plan to pray for and get involved in your community to make a difference in 2019.
* * *
# Pakistan Government Stops Asia Bibi From Fleeing Country
The Pakistani government has been widely criticized for striking a deal with radical Islamic clerics and agreeing to prevent Christian mother Asia Bibi from leaving the country, despite her Supreme Court acquittal for blasphemy. The action, taken by Prime Minister Imran Khan in response to increasingly explosive protests at the ruling, will entail the pursuit of legal proceedings to place Asia Bibi on the “exit control list” (ECL). Khan appealed for calm earlier in the week and accused radicals of doing “no service to Islam” by protesting the final judgment, but his words seemed only to embolden the furious crowds. The demonstrations continued to swell on Friday as thousands of hardliners associated with extremist party Tehreek-e-Labbaik [Pakistan](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=H-qEORLjmEZ0R4IEh6LC5d17xnsSw8TUPE-GhGG_GjXg4EnsHliWrUOazMtFElYi&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181031intersect01&n=8) party (TLP) took to the streets and demanded that the court reconsiders its verdict. _[read more](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=M3gmYtQVGobEnoHfgg7VNdRsV1gNKLurh5PE2rJxvz3tjLV6NqVWO2CGj7k06bsC6GAXA9EoxNdtg0OD5BB4FDW6djOgPFpIsOhWYy7R_09ENFkNWi9NAy-yYQ5u6-WGVoYNZL85wbhBxmnnQs46wv-mX75am2R7Irz29VqMc5k&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181031intersect01&n=9)_ **THINK.**
On Sunday Asia Bibi’s husband appealed to President Trump and British Prime Minister Theresa May to help his family leave Pakistan. Bibi's lawyer Saiful Malook has already fled the country for safety. All Christians in Pakistan are under extreme threat during this time as Islamic leaders threaten violence. Where freedom of religion exists, prosperity abounds. But where Christianity is lived out, persecution will follow. Jesus promised it, Paul highlighted it, and Peter said don’t be surprised by it (John 16:33, 2 Timothy 3:12, 1 Peter 4:12). Christians in Pakistan are often treated as second-class citizens. 95 percent of Pakistanis identify as Muslim and only 1.6 percent identify as Christian. It is a crime punishable by death in Pakistan to insult Islam. Sunday was International Day of Prayer for the Persecuted Church. The Voice of the Martyrs put together a moving video on a church in Pakistan, “[Fassal: Pakistan](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=Dyqugx0OxkmOtMuRuicpIqpvFZ_vXDyw2WYAWJT9ZoP7ZdCH-2gMDijT9FehHQam&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181031intersect01&n=10).” Watch the trailer and consider hosting a gathering at your church to show the movie. **PRAY.**
Please pray for Asia Bibi’s family as they try to escape the country. And pray for all of the Christians in Pakistan that God would protect them and that they would have sustained strength to remain faithful under the most severe persecution as they fear for their lives. **ACT.**
Sometimes it’s hard to know how to pray for our brothers and sisters in Christ who are experiencing extreme persecution that we have not experienced in America. Take a few minutes to read this article, [4 Ways to Pray for Persecuted Christians](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=M3gmYtQVGobEnoHfgg7VNdRsV1gNKLurh5PE2rJxvz2oKG9YXuy8-OdE5Smk9YKdh2F6FMtWQtAhFkrakBBR4bNFGcJV2Aimu1ZnA-vlFbM&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181031intersect01&n=11), to lift up the persecuted church in prayer.
* * *
# Trainee Kills Utah Mayor, Guard Member in Afghanistan
A Utah mayor who was also a Utah Army National Guard Major training commandos in Afghanistan was fatally shot by one of his Afghan trainees, officials said Sunday. Brent Taylor, 39, had taken a yearlong leave of absence as mayor of North Ogden north of Salt Lake City for his deployment to Afghanistan. He was a military intelligence officer with Joint Force Headquarters and was expected to return to his mayoral job in January. Another U.S. military member whose name was not immediately made public was wounded in Saturday's attack that killed Taylor, who died from wounds from small arms fire, military officials said. Maj. Gen. Jefferson S. Burton, the adjutant general of the Utah National Guard, told reporters that Taylor's mission was to help train and build the capacity of the Afghan national army. "He was with folks he was helping and training. That's what's so painful about this. It's bitter," Burton said. "I do believe that Major Taylor felt he was among friends, with people he was working with." _[read more](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=KVYzWTFUuJ0SsyVZLAg-7BA78zf_Nlo3NKCDttvT6pe_SEK7bs9vRFgDPjWCfZS-YCd8SPQevXebynrCp1MzVjRJz3YrIhXxyRj9hqz-FIY&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181031intersect01&n=12)_ **THINK.**
Since 2013, Taylor had served as Mayor of North Ogden, a town of 17,000 people outside of Salt Lake City, Utah. He had served two tours in Iraq and was on his second tour in Afghanistan. Mayor Taylor leaves behind a wife and seven young children. When Taylor left Utah for Afghanistan, North Ogden school children lined the streets and cheered for him as he waved from a motorcade. There are approximately [8,400 troops stationed](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=d2MaO5qc1nNfqTNlKbNR2duChkYkHQZOVZs7PS2w1VreNDjvGMJs2owHKVWpID3y5fMgBuFAcOWxV0YW6ehmTpk_WXTGyYbmM8iQ3i3RxmDpTrpIbe3Z0AQrhTDM3bSr&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181031intersect01&n=13) in Afghanistan. When Mayor Taylor announced his deployment to Afghanistan in a Facebook video [he said](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=gIX-ooClgO9ktCs4vE4abNU4qvjyIiDAAgzG3ZC69O_nusgMewdZyhJjXQ_7YHsTgJGuuRkkJhN6jZmbPGscnyUD-B0dovpbCL8aKVohYo7JU_W5LBZjOzgVjoUFJpjE28RmxVSeQWofBcBRMaiTng&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181031intersect01&n=14), "There are three great loyalties that have guided my life and everything in it: God, family, and country. While I am far from perfect in any of these respects, I have given my life to serve all three of these loyalties whenever and however I can, and right now there is a need for my experiences and skills to serve in our nation's long-lasting war in Afghanistan." And the last week of October he posted another message on Facebook about Afghanistan’s first parliamentary election in eight years. He commended those who turned out to vote despite the "threats and deadly attacks." He continued with a message for America, “I hope everyone back home exercises their precious right to vote. And that whether the Republicans or the Democrats win, that we all remember that we have far more as Americans that unites us than divides us. ‘United we stand, divided we fall.’ God Bless America.” **PRAY.**
Pray for Mayor Taylor’s family, his wife and his seven children during this terrible time of mourning (James 5:13, Ephesians 6:18). And pray for protection for our troops in Afghanistan as they continue to put their lives on the line in service to our country. **ACT.**
This Sunday, November 11th, is Veterans Day, a time to honor and celebrate our men and women who have served in our military. Different than Memorial Day, Veterans Day honors all of those who have served the country in war or peace — dead or alive — although it’s largely intended to thank living veterans for their sacrifices. Take the time to thank a veteran in your life. Also, take a moment to learn more about the important work of [REBOOT](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FOBL-FhWcBmU7d8w1E7cMM31fkgQSYpLoi0s3DDpQUs&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181031intersect01&n=15), an organization utilizing faith-based trauma healing courses designed to help combat veterans and their families recover from the spiritual and moral wounds of war.
* * *
# One in Three College Students Believe Physical Violence is Justified to Prevent ‘Hate Speech’
The majority of college students favor banning speakers who use "hate speech", with a third justifying the use of violence to prevent such rhetoric from happening, a new survey has found. A majority of students, 53%, say they feel “intimidated” to share their ideas, opinions, or beliefs in the classroom because they are not the same as their professors, while a slightly larger majority fears expressing themselves because of differences of opinion with classmates.” _[read more](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=KVYzWTFUuJ0SsyVZLAg-7KGe7dQTydayNUguVR7oWbydQNa82SWcgrZohX1Py8-fOQSzqNGoMC3reM_dg05jI-RTot2rTjtk69PhoruAOX-qs2HQ7iO96Zo8IW4cvskmsE_lOX0ZOYEX5IT9GaZEFKMELVnJMXOoF1piLpN7Q7U&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181031intersect01&n=16)_ **THINK.**
In the book, The Coddling of the American Mind, Jon Haidt and Greg Lukianoff note that this is largely due to a culture of safetyism. In such a culture, concept creep has crept in, equating physical harm to emotional harm. While safety against physical threats is a positive good, safety against emotional threats turns into a feedback loop. Students become more fragile and less resilient, requiring more protection. Jean Twenge, in her book iGen, writes: “18-year-olds now act like 15-year-olds used to and 13-year-olds like 10-year-olds. Teens are physically safer than ever, yet they are more mentally vulnerable.” As Christians, we should listen quickly, reason respectfully, and be confident that truth always wins the day (James 1:19, Isaiah 1:18, Matthew 16:18). **PRAY.**
Pray for the next generation today on University campuses across our country. Pray for them to come to know Christ as their personal Lord and Savior and to walk in His love and grace. And pray for our Christian young people to stand strong in defense of Christian values. **ACT.**
Read this encouraging article about a recent victory by Alliance Defending Freedom in Georgia where a [Christian Apologetics Club won a free speech battle](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=HTIUaYWGgtW-Sq8jttULzX61HryB4MfdillBHImjj_kuU5ecLDfVtjtOJCgYZGS092Y_-sEcSc7zTnLaIZVS25mZ6ZpXUHUfcIDCmBs5chBBWq_e8zDnXkSSU70bB7apB0Tu8BoHTC17PGzaMLCzaBC87VsdYO1LSMt-6UxINAs&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181031intersect01&n=17) on a State University campus.

Another U.S. military member whose name was not immediately made public was wounded in Saturday's attack that killed Taylor, who died from wounds from small arms fire, military officials said. Maj. Gen. Jefferson S. Burton, the adjutant general of the Utah National Guard, told reporters that Taylor's mission was to help train and build the capacity of the Afghan national army. "He was with folks he was helping and training. That's what's so painful about this. It's bitter," Burton said. "I do believe that Major Taylor felt he was among friends, with people he was working with." THINK. Since 2013, Taylor had served as Mayor of North Ogden, a town of 17,000 people outside of Salt Lake City, Utah. He had served two tours in Iraq and was on his second tour in Afghanistan. Mayor Taylor leaves behind a wife and seven young children. When Taylor left Utah for Afghanistan, North Ogden school children lined the streets and cheered for him as he waved from a motorcade. There are approximately in Afghanistan. When Mayor Taylor announced his deployment to Afghanistan in a Facebook video , "There are three great loyalties that have guided my life and everything in it: God, family, and country. While I am far from perfect in any of these respects, I have given my life to serve all three of these loyalties whenever and however I can, and right now there is a need for my experiences and skills to serve in our nation's long-lasting war in Afghanistan." And the last week of October he posted another message on Facebook about Afghanistan’s first parliamentary election in eight years. He commended those who turned out to vote despite the "threats and deadly attacks." He continued with a message for America, “I hope everyone back home exercises their precious right to vote. And that whether the Republicans or the Democrats win, that we all remember that we have far more as Americans that unites us than divides us. ‘United we stand, divided we fall.’ God Bless America.” PRAY. Pray for Mayor Taylor’s family, his wife and his seven children during this terrible time of mourning (James 5:13, Ephesians 6:18). And pray for protection for our troops in Afghanistan as they continue to put their lives on the line in service to our country. ACT. This Sunday, November 11th, is Veterans Day, a time to honor and celebrate our men and women who have served in our military. Different than Memorial Day, Veterans Day honors all of those who have served the country in war or peace — dead or alive — although it’s largely intended to thank living veterans for their sacrifices. Take the time to thank a veteran in your life. Also, take a moment to learn more about the important work of , an organization utilizing faith-based trauma healing courses designed to help combat veterans and their families recover from the spiritual and moral wounds of war. One in Three College Students Believe Physical Violence is Justified to Prevent ‘Hate Speech’ The majority of college students favor banning speakers who use "hate speech", with a third justifying the use of violence to prevent such rhetoric from happening, a new survey has found. A majority of students, 53%, say they feel “intimidated” to share their ideas, opinions, or beliefs in the classroom because they are not the same as their professors, while a slightly larger majority fears expressing themselves because of differences of opinion with classmates.” THINK. In the book, The Coddling of the American Mind, Jon Haidt and Greg Lukianoff note that this is largely due to a culture of safetyism. In such a culture, concept creep has crept in, equating physical harm to emotional harm. While safety against physical threats is a positive good, safety against emotional threats turns into a feedback loop. Students become more fragile and less resilient, requiring more protection. Jean Twenge, in her book iGen, writes: “18-year-olds now act like 15-year-olds used to and 13-year-olds like 10-year-olds. Teens are physically safer than ever, yet they are more mentally vulnerable.” As Christians, we should listen quickly, reason respectfully, and be confident that truth always wins the day (James 1:19, Isaiah 1:18, Matthew 16:18). PRAY. Pray for the next generation today on University campuses across our country. Pray for them to come to know Christ as their personal Lord and Savior and to walk in His love and grace. And pray for our Christian young people to stand strong in defense of Christian values. ACT. Read this encouraging article about a recent victory by Alliance Defending Freedom in Georgia where a on a State University campus. If receiving the Intersect is a blessing to you, so that we can continue to encourage more Christians to think, pray and act! ©2018 My Faith Votes 10940 S Parker, 640, Parker, CO 80134

body:
Peter,

I am so grateful to continue representing the hardworking
communities of California’s 5th district in Congress. I can’t
thank you enough for your support.

Last night was a win for Democrats as we took back the House
majority, and it’s all because of your hard work, dedication, and
incredible grassroots energy. We should all be proud of the great
work we’ve done mobilizing a record number of engaged voters
across California and the country.

I look forward to getting back to work in Congress fighting
for affordable health care, gun violence prevention,
environmental protections and California’s natural resources.

Thank you again for your tireless support throughout the ups
and downs of this election. You called, knocked doors, and made
your voice heard loud and clear.

Our work doesn’t stop here. Let’s keep this energy going.

Onward,

Mike

represents California's 5th district in Congress, where he fights
for our healthcare and a clean environment every day. He cannot
continue to do his crucial work without the support of people

But there’s no padded room to keep them contained! Instead, they’re leftist indoctrination factories that punish dissenting speech and ban views that don’t align with the socialists, communists, and Marxists trying to hijack our country. I fear for our future when colleges teach our nation’s youth to harass, harangue, and harm anyone who disagrees with them — or even anyone designated a Bad Thinker by people who can’t think at all. Instead of a battle of ideas, they’re being taught to do real battle — to riot in the streets with black masks and baseball bats. What harm did all those Starbucks windows ever do to them? Young conservatives on college campuses across our nation are losing their right to free speech – and even the right to walk down the street without getting sucker punched – because of how intolerant the liberals have become. But lest you think I’ve given up hope, faithful conservative, there’s [a group I trust that fights back](http://trk.listelixr.net/c/4/?T=OTkyNjA2OTc%3AMDItYjE4MzExLTk5YTlmNjNkMDY3MjQyYTE4ZmJmMTliZGI4YWI4Mzkw%3AZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20%3AMjgyNTQ5Mjg%3AZmFsc2U%3ANA%3A%3AaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmVkLmxlYWRlcnNoaXBpbnN0aXR1dGUub3JnLzIwMTgtMTAtY291bHRlci8_dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1nb3BwcmVzaWRlbnRpYWwzJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPTIwMThjb3VsdGVyJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PTIwMTgxMTA1X2NvdWx0ZXJfZ29wMyZhcHBlYWw9ZWFjYjAwOA&K=Gwvg2sN3TY9AMnnYyOoC5w) against this onslaught and exposes leftist bias, abuse, and indoctrination on college campuses for all the world to see: Campus Reform. **Campus Reform, a project of the Leadership Institute, is the single most powerful force for campus free speech in America today.** By sticking up for conservative students and giving them the tools necessary to defend themselves and their principles, Campus Reform provides an absolutely essential public service. **Bottom line – Campus Reform uncovers the liberal schemes that the mainstream media wants to keep hidden . . . and they shut down the liberals’ indoctrination on campus after campus.** **[To continue their vital work, Campus Reform counts on the generous support of conservatives just like you – yes, you! – which is why I urge you to partner with them today by making a generous gift of $25, $35, $50, $100, or more right now.](http://trk.listelixr.net/c/4/?T=OTkyNjA2OTc%3AMDItYjE4MzExLTk5YTlmNjNkMDY3MjQyYTE4ZmJmMTliZGI4YWI4Mzkw%3AZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20%3AMjgyNTQ5Mjg%3AZmFsc2U%3ANQ%3A%3AaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmVkLmxlYWRlcnNoaXBpbnN0aXR1dGUub3JnLzIwMTgtMTAtY291bHRlci8_dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1nb3BwcmVzaWRlbnRpYWwzJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPTIwMThjb3VsdGVyJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PTIwMTgxMTA1X2NvdWx0ZXJfZ29wMyZhcHBlYWw9ZWFjYjAwOA&K=jY-1ddZNbs2CuRayUGKtug)** And to sweeten the pot – when you give $100 or more to LI’s Campus Reform, I’ll rush you a copy of my new book, _Resistance is Futile._ Saving young conservatives AND getting a free book? What a deal! But let me tell you more. Campus Reform is run by Morton Blackwell’s Leadership Institute. LI is one of the nation’s most respected and powerful voices for conservative students and young people. They’ve organized and trained a national network of 2,083 active conservative student groups at colleges all over America. **I took one of the Leadership Institute’s courses myself when I was a student. I** t helped me become a better editor and expose the liberals tricks at my college, in addition to cheering me up by introducing me to other young patriots. Like they did for me, LI trains these students to powerfully — and civilly! — articulate conservative principles. Their training equips young people with the tools to stand up for their principles when they find themselves censored, punished, or threatened by radical faculty or students. You see, it’s not just media personalities like Ben Shapiro, James O’Keefe, and myself that the liberal mob tries to run off campuses. Campus police at the College of DuPage in Illinois told conservative students they’d **“lock them up” for handing out copies of the U.S. Constitution.** Yes, that dangerous document that the created the greatest country ever known to man. A teaching assistant at the University of Nebraska-Lincoln harassed the student president of a conservative group for recruiting students. With a gesture from her middle finger, she shrieked profanities at the conservative student and called her a “neo-fascist” and “Nazi.” Charming. At Purdue University in Indiana, a staffer called conservatives “vile, racist idiots” and **threatened to rape pro-life students.** (And you wonder why liberals prefer violence to debate.) In all three cases, LI’s Campus Reform exposed the truth. Armed with investigative reports from their student correspondents on campus, Campus Reform got word out to their nationwide audience of concerned conservatives. And they brought it to news outlets like Fox News, Newsmax, the Blaze, and numerous talk radio shows. Ultimately, Campus Reform’s exposure of the truth got _results_. In these cases and many others, Campus Reform’s quick and powerful action forced universities to change their anti-conservative policies, discipline leftist student groups, and suspend or fire radical professors. I believe an angel gets its wings every time a leftist professor is fired for their outrageous and unapologetic abuses against conservatives. But Campus Reform’s fight to make the left see the light and feel the heat won’t be won overnight. And they brought it to news outlets like Fox News, Newsmax, the Blaze, and numerous talk radio shows. Ultimately, Campus Reform’s exposure of the truth got results. In these cases and many others, Campus Reform’s quick and powerful action forced universities to change their anti-conservative policies, discipline leftist student groups, and suspend or fire radical professors. I believe an angel gets its wings every time a leftist professor is fired for their outrageous and unapologetic abuses against conservatives. But Campus Reform’s fight to make the left see the light and feel the heat won’t be won overnight. To fight the forces of the intolerant left, stand up for free speech, and protect conservative students who want to simply share their beliefs without getting a black eye, Campus Reform is counting on your help. | The Stalinist show-trial that liberals tried to hold against Judge Brett Kavanaugh reveals the disgusting depths the radical left will stoop to. name: =?utf-8?Q?Ian=20Todd?=
subject: We may not have won, but we took an important step forward.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY IAN TODD FOR CONGRESS
body:
** This is just halftime!
------------------------------------------------------------
We knew from the beginning that this would be a challenging race, and that is why from the start of this campaign I pledged to run again no matter what. I am proud of what we accomplished yesterday. Candidates for the state legislature picked up nearly 10 points in two critical districts (35A and 35B) and our campaign brought this district from R+31 to R+22, all while we were outspent 27 to1!

I can not thank you all enough for all of the volunteer hours and support you have given me over the past year. Without you, this campaign would not have been possible. So now, I am holding up my pledge to you to run again because I believe that given more time to prepare and do the hard organizing work that needs to be done, we can win this in 2020.

I hope you all take some time to enjoy the holidays as they come and I hope we can all take a minute to celebrate the progressive victories that happened nationwide last night. In the meantime, I am asking you to set up a recurring contribution to my campaign so that we can head into the 2020 election well funded and well prepared. Even a small amount over the course of 2 years could make all the difference.

Thank you so much for your support.

Your friend,

Ian A. Todd

name: Steve Sisolak
subject: Thanks to you
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY FRIENDS FOR STEVE SISOLAK |
body:
Dear Friend,

We did it.

I’m so grateful to supporters like you for making this victory possible. This race was neck and neck for months, and we never would have won without your help.

Nevadans stood up and declared that it is time to bring people together, to prioritize our schools, our health care, and our jobs — and to get things done. As governor, that is exactly what I will do.

Today, we’re celebrating, but tomorrow I’m ready to get to work for all Nevadans as your next governor. Because this is about more than Democrat or Republican, Northern or Southern, urban or rural — it's about giving every family the chance to succeed.

And I’m asking for you to stay involved to keep this movement going because we’ve got a lot of work to do, together.

Thank you,

Steve Sisolak

subject: THANK YOU!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Gina Raimondo

| | Hi friend, Thank you! I am so humbled and honored to continue serving as Governor of Rhode Island. In four years, we've brought real change: we've created opportunity and invested in job training, and we've taken Rhode Island's economy from having the highest unemployment rate in America to having more jobs than ever before in the state's history. And as proud as I am about all we've done, there is still work to do. There are more jobs than ever before, but the recovery still hasn't reached everyone. And we're not going to stop until every Rhode Islander is included. We won't stop until every child is lifted out of poverty; until every child can attend a flourishing public school; until we meet the needs of every senior; and until every Rhode Islander has a shot at a good job. The campaign is over. It's time to put away the lawn signs, and it's time to come together as Rhode Islanders. I am certain that no matter the rhetoric in Washington, that Rhode Islanders of all faiths, of all races, of all political affiliations will always rise above division. I will need your help over the next four years to protect the change and progress we've made, and I intend to continue governing for every Rhode Islander. Thank you for this opportunity to serve. I promise to wake up every morning to work for you. \- Gina

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Americans Aren’t Just Happy About the Economy
body:
"The President elected two years ago to revive American prosperity is now presiding over an economic boom." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8748402c0c&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# Americans Aren’t Just Happy About the Economy

“The President elected two years ago to revive American prosperity is now presiding over an economic boom. The first two years of the Trump era have been so good that his predecessor has lately been barnstorming the country trying to persuade voters that we’re still living in the Obama era. It is the sincerest form of political flattery,” James Freeman writes in _The Wall Street Journal_.

[Click here to continue reading.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5f44d55899&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| Republicans expanded their majority in the U.S. Senate in yesterday’s midterm elections, “riding a Trump train fueled by red-state enthusiasm,” David M. Drucker writes in the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3f5ad4512d&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Our president has made such a difference already, he’s energized the Republican Party again. It’s so exciting to see that happen and he’s making a difference all across the board,” one Tennessee voter said.
---
|
---
| “The ratio of unemployed workers to open jobs hovered at a record low in September” according to the Department of Labor, Joseph Lawler reports for the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9b7f6c4545&e=3ee4985eea)_. “There were 0.9 unemployed workers for every job vacancy listed in the month, a ratio the agency said was the lowest since it's been keeping track and that hasn't been recorded before this year.”
---
|
---
| In [CNBC](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5efcc7b31e&e=3ee4985eea), Michael Sheetz writes that service-sector growth for October was strong and the economy remains hot. “The data release also came after ADP and Moody's Analytics reported [last] Wednesday better-than-expected payrolls creation for October. The two firms said . . . private companies added 227,000 payrolls last month.”
---
|
---
| “Mexico launched a ‘Make Yourself at Home’ program, offering migrants shelter, food, work and schools for their kids. Most turned it down,” Betsy McCaughey in the _[New York Post](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7ee458536b&e=3ee4985eea)_. The reality is that most migrants in the caravan headed toward America’s border are in search of a lifestyle upgrade, not fleeing genuine violence or persecution.
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=311f2e38fe&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7a9032bf93&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=6ed2512684)

body:

---
Friend,

WE DID IT.

**Last night, thanks to supporters like you, we broke the Republican supermajorities in BOTH the State House and the State Senate and we overwhelmingly rejected the power grabbing constitutional amendments.**

When we set out with Break the Majority last year one thing was clear: we needed a legislature that would work with us on the most important priorities for our state.

Over the last year and a half, Break the Majority built one of the best field organizations this state has ever seen. We fought for every single vote -- neighborhood by neighborhood. And it paid off.

This wouldn't be possible without the hard work from all of the organizers and volunteers. Supporters like you paved the way for this. Your hard work will impact North Carolina for generations to come and I'm so proud to stand with you.

The people of this state showed they want a legislature willing to work with the Governor to raise teacher pay, create good paying jobs, strengthen our health care system by expanding Medicaid, train our workforce and help communities devastated by recent storms to rebuild.

**We have a lot to do. Let's get to work. **

name: Mel Hall
subject: Thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mel Hall for Congress |
body:
Friend,

The results are in, and unfortunately, it’s not the outcome we were hoping for.

Even though we came up just short last night, Hoosiers across the 2nd District sent a message loud and clear yesterday: it’s time for a change in the way Washington operates.

I’m proud of the campaign we ran, and the people we met along the way. Most importantly, I’m so grateful for all your hard work and support along the way.

Folks across the district are eager for a representative who will protect their health care, grow good-paying jobs, and clean up the partisan mess in Congress. While that person will not be me, I am so encouraged by the enthusiasm and the appetite for change I saw across all ten counties of this district.

Thank you very much for being part of this campaign,

Mel

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: Thank you, Peter! We did it!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
body:
This is your victory. This campaign could not have succeeded without you -- our tremendous network of volunteers and grassroots supporters who came together to organize and stand up for our values.
| | **Thank you, Peter!_We did it!_** When we launched this campaign from my kitchen more than a year ago, conventional wisdom dictated that this race was unwinnable. It was impossible, some said, to win a district that a Democrat hadn't won since 1968. **But we won an unwinnable district last night by doing what every campaign should do:** We focused on the needs of the people in this community and we talked about the substantive issues affecting their lives. We stood up for American values, and we brought respect and decency back to the political process. This campaign has always been about giving people something to vote _for._ We are for a health care system that is affordable and accessible for all; for safe, nurturing, well-funded public schools; for bipartisanship and a Congress committed to serving the people, not special interests; and so much more. **We offered families in Virginia 's 7th district something to vote for, Peter, and yesterday, they entrusted me with serving them in Congress.** And I say we, because this is your victory. This campaign could not have succeeded without you -- our tremendous network of volunteers and grassroots supporters who came together to organize, stand up for our values, and talk to voters about the issues at stake in this race. You inspire me, and I am sincerely humbled and honored that you have placed your faith in me, Peter. Now, we will take this fight for Virginia families to Congress. Thank you! \- Abigail _Abigail Spanberger is a proud Democrat and former CIA officer running for Congress against Dave Brat in Virginia 's 7th Congressional District. She believes in leadership that flows from our shared American values of integrity, responsibility, and decency, and she wants to work to move this country forward together. To learn more, please visit [AbigailSpanberger.com](https://act.myngp.com/el/TmneQw3pZ_t04iHpTc-tYh_QUURxYwEw1l_5RpAWYlw=/ciunwxZ5a9k9L0Tbb-JC0WW_PziQX4uyb5MBeMQ-6RI=)_

name: Ralph
subject: From the bottom of my heart, thank you, friends
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Way Ahead The Way Ahead
body:
| | |
---
Friends--

These elections were about the character of our country--I'm proud that Virginians once again rejected the politics of division that are tearing our country apart. We've elected leaders who'll fight for every Virginian, regardless of who they are or where they come from, and work together with everyone to do what's best for the commonwealth.
We have elected strong candidates like Jennifer Wexton, Elaine Luria, and Abigail Spanberger. Your door knocks, your calls, your donations, and your dedication and tenacity have made a difference.**None of this happens unless we decide to make it happen. Thank you for showing up, turning out, and working hard.**
Let's also congratulate Anthony Flaccavento, Jennifer Lewis, Leslie Cockburn, and Vangie Williams for their valiant campaign efforts. I'm proud of these candidates and the campaigns they ran. Public service is difficult, but it has never been more important. They put their names forward and can be proud of the campaigns they ran. Also, congratulations to Don McEachin, Don Beyer, Bobby Scott, and Gerry Connolly on their reelection. And, I am so proud that Virginians have sent Tim Kaine back to the US Senate for another term.
Together, we're doing things the Virginia Way--working hard to do the right thing and get things done--and it's paying off like we've never seen.
Today, we celebrate our victories, and tomorrow we get straight back to work on making Virginia a better place for working families. Let's get this done and pave the way ahead for a future filled with hope and prosperity.
Take care,

--Ralph

body:
66 candidates in 27 states fighting for the political revolution went up against a system rigged with voter suppression and dark money - and with your help - won.
#
**66 candidates** in **27 states** fighting for the political revolution went up against a system rigged with voter suppression and dark money - and with your help - **won.**

**[The Democrats who took back the House of Representatives and progressive champions at the state and local level are ready to make real change. Let's make sure the progressive platforms they ran on become policies that uplift working families.](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a714/bed43eb/8cdaa216/75fc4a58/3566051628/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-historic?refcode=em181107-d&noskip=true")**

**[DONATE»](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a714/bed43eb/8cdaa216/75fc4a58/3566051628/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-historic?refcode=em181107-d&noskip=true")**
Friends,
Many of the progressive candidates you supported beat out the divisive and dangerous Republican agenda last night. Take a look at just a few of last night's victories:
**Florida passed Amendment 4 and restored the right to vote for 1.5 million citizens** who have paid their debt to society, creating the single largest expansion of voters by a policy since the Voting Rights Act of 1965.
**Andrew Gillum's run for governor** in the great state of Florida also helped to expand the electorate by over a million people and softened the soil for the next progressive champion.
**Ilhan Omar and Rashida Tlaib became the first Muslim women elected to Congress.** They will work to pass Medicare for All, a $15 minimum wage and public higher education for all. We now have a substantial block of serious progressives in the House of Representatives who will hold establishment Democrats accountable to the people.
**Marc Elrich was elected Montgomery County Executive in Maryland.** The one million voters in Montgomery County supported this progressive leader who was fundamental in building Our Revolution Maryland, a force of grassroots activists and community leaders who are building independent political power. This victory will have an enormous impact on the state of affordable housing, public schools, and wages in this community.
**[Donate $5 right now to our grassroots movement so we can turn revolutionary platforms into revolutionary policy.](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a714/bed43eb/8cdaa216/75fc4a58/3566051628/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-historic?refcode=em181107-d&noskip=true")**
Our work is just beginning. With so much on the line, we need to build a grassroots movement to support elected officials who are fighting for policies that make investments in _you, your family and your community._
**With your support, we were able to send over 4.3 million peer-to-peer text messages and organized with over 600 local Our Revolution groups - in all 50 states - to ensure we reached voters.**
By reclaiming our democracy from wealthy special interests we will make tuition-free public higher education available to everyone, regardless of how much they make. We will make health care a right, not a privilege. We will make clean drinking water non-negotiable. Without the shadows of dark money casting down on our government, we will pass progressive policies that change people's lives.
**[Make a $5 donation so we can build the movement to support these policies and the extraordinary people you elected last night. The political revolution is catching fire because of you.](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a714/bed43eb/8cdaa216/75fc4a58/3566051628/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-historic?refcode=em181107-d&noskip=true")**
Thank you for everything you have done to make these victories possible. On behalf of our entire team, we could not have done it without you.
In solidarity,
Nina Turner
President
Our Revolution

name: Joe Donnelly
subject: Thank you, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
I'm proud of the values that our campaign was based on: That every citizen deserves access to quality, affordable health care. That we must keep our promise to our seniors by protecting Social Securit |
---
[]()

| | Peter,

Before I say anything else, I want to thank you from the bottom of my heart for everything you've done for this campaign over the past 14 months.

Yesterday's outcome was not the one we were hoping for. But I truly believe that together, we made a difference.

I'm proud of the values that our campaign was based on: That every citizen deserves access to quality, affordable health care. That we must keep our promise to our seniors by protecting Social Security and Medicare. That our veterans deserve nothing but the best care and support. That every person who wants a good-paying job should be able to find one.

Together, you and I made our support for those values loud and clear. Now I'm relying on you to keep standing up for them, to keep using your voice to fight for the people of Indiana and America in these challenging times.

I want to congratulate my opponent, Mike Braun. I hope that he will serve the people of Indiana to the best of his ability.

Serving you has been one of the greatest honors of my life. I'm proud of what we were able to accomplish together.

Thanks for everything,
Joe

|

subject: Thank you
body:

| |

| Friend,
---
| There are few higher honors than being elected by your neighbors; for them to have enough faith and trust in you to represent them and their interests; and be the person they are counting on to work on the issues that are most important to them.

And there’s no greater responsibility than working – every day – to understand and solve the problems facing our state. It is the greatest honor of my life to have the trust of Vermonters to carry out this work on their behalf as Governor, and I humbly accept that challenge once again.

So, from the bottom of my heart, thank you. Let’s continue to build a more affordable and prosperous Vermont, together.
Sincerely,

Phil Scott

name: Jason Yates
subject: 6 insights to the Midterm Elections
body:
|
---
| Peter, In the beginning of the year, we worked with some research that indicated there might be as many as 50 million Christians who would not vote in 2018. I’m glad to report, thanks to you and millions of Christians across the country, that did not happen. I am so appreciative for all of you who not only went to the polls and cast your vote, but also encouraged others at your church, in your families, and in your community to do the same. I’m overwhelmed by the response and engagement we have seen to the voting resources provided by My Faith Votes, and it gives me every confidence that we are making an impact. So, whether you have donated, accessed a voter guide, or shared our resources with a friend, thank you for what you have done. I’ve been pouring over the results last night and this morning, and I want to provide you with some [insights that you may not have yet received](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iRZRAG9h8vIS7MA9uqg52n8_aEdc3RCmNldFidQtyalAbuhkmW09kzpgusJ1HQYs25DkEIj5EMVk0oOhg_qJpYQ&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181107com01&n=1). name: Scholten Election Team
subject: It’s been one hell of a ride.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scholten4Iowa Campaign Committee
body:
Peter,
It’s been one hell of a ride.
In June of last year, I met who became the first two members of my staff, and I told them, “There are two things that make me uncomfortable: speaking in public, and asking people for money; so naturally, I should run for Congress.” In the 17 months since that day, what this campaign, this movement, has accomplished, is nothing short of remarkable.
Thank you.
To my staff. To our interns. To our wonderful volunteers. To the organizations that endorsed and supported us. To all of you that have been a part of this incredibly special movement.
Thanks to you, we raised over $3.2 million dollars from over 55,000 individual donors.
Thanks to you, we produced the greatest field program this district has ever seen, making tens of thousands of calls and texts, and knocking on thousands of doors.
Thanks to you, our posts and our videos were shared literally millions of times on social media.
Thanks to you, this election was the biggest story in the country for the final week before Election Day.
Thanks to you, we took a race that Steve King had won by 24 and 22 percentage points in the last two cycles and closed that gap to just 3.
Thanks to you, we received over 25,000 more votes than there are registered Democrats in this district.
Thanks to you, someone told me last night that this was the first time in his life that people walked into an election night watch party against Steve King and didn’t know what was going to happen.
Thanks to you, people in the smallest of towns felt like they had a voice too.
Thanks to you, people in this district found something we hadn’t seen in a generation. **Hope.**
It’s been one hell of a ride.
It wasn’t easy, but I called Steve King last night, and I told him that I would pray for him, and that I prayed that he would represent this district responsibly and respectfully. It’s up to us now to make sure that he does, because if he doesn’t, we’ll make sure this is the last time he ever celebrates an election night victory.
I modeled this campaign after two of my political heroes, Berkley Bedell and Tom Harkin. They both made it to Congress after losing in their first attempts. Let’s just say that this isn’t the last you’ve heard of J.D. Scholten.
It has been an honor to share this journey with all of you.
My name is J.D. Scholten, and I am still Standing Tall for All.

name: Stephanie Schriock, EMILY's List
subject: A historic election
disclaimer_text: Paid for by EMILY's List [www
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118cb92c/24c69fd9/2116a0c4d/18bf55a2/2992139375/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFEW44N_digital_email_20181107_2018Reportback_inact&source=WQEFEW44N_digital_email_20181107_2018Reportback_inact")
---

Peter,

After the 2016 election, women across the country courageously responded to the devastating presidential results by running for elected office in record numbers.

**And yesterday, hundreds of EMILY's List-endorsed women won.**

Thanks to our women's victories, we took back the House! There are more races still to be called, but so far, 21 of the 23 seats Democrats needed to flip to win the majority were won by our women. That will be huge in holding the Trump administration accountable.

We also TRIPLED the number of pro-choice Democratic women governors in the country, creating a strong defense against the dangerous anti-choice Republican agenda at the state level.

And down the ballot, hundreds of our state and local women won -- Democrats flipped six state legislative chambers, broke up Republican supermajorities in four, and gained Democratic supermajorities in two.

**In short: EMILY's List women changed the face of government last night.**

Here's a closer look at our historic wins:
**SENATE:**

So far, 10 of our candidates won, including **Jacky Rosen** (NV), who flipped a seat from red to blue. She joins nine of our women who were up for re-election and won.

**HOUSE:**

Thirty-eight of our women have won so far, including 21 of our candidates who were running to flip seats and who helped turn the House blue! Among those wins are so many historic firsts, like the first Native American and Muslim women elected to Congress.

**GOVERNORS:**

Six of our candidates have won so far, including four women who flipped seats. **Michelle Lujan Grisham** (NM) was one of those flips, and she will be the first Democratic Hispanic woman governor in the country.

**STATE & LOCAL:**

And more than 250 of our state and local women won their elections, creating an incredible pipeline of strong candidates who have bright careers ahead of them and who will _continue_ to change the face of power for years to come.
We'll keep you posted as more races are called, but after last night, one thing is already clear: A historic number of women who will defend our rights will be sworn in in January.

_That_ is the power of this community.

And I couldn't be more proud.

Thank you for joining the fight,

Stephanie Schriock
President, EMILY's List

Our work doesn't stop just because Election Day is over. Some of our races are still too close to call or heading into runoffs, and we're going to keep working to elect women next year and for years to come.

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: Thank you
body:
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb35c0/9e00690/694cedb5/2400a059/2533116292/VEsH/ "https://andrewgillum.com/")
Friends,

>From the bottom of my heart, I want to thank you for being part of this campaign. I will be forever grateful for your support and for your belief in our shared vision of moving our state forward.

This was a hard-fought race. We didn't get the outcome we hoped for last night -- but we know that over the course of the last 20 months, we have changed our state and country forever. As the race tightens and votes continue to be counted, we are watching very closely to ensure that all Floridians have the opportunity to have their voices heard.

I still have the same goal: to build this state into one that works better for everyday Floridians.

My grandmother taught me how to stiffen my spine and keep myself in the fight. And now, as I lean on her words, I want you to do the same.

We still have to be willing to show up every single day and demand our seat at the table. I still believe and I still trust in the voters. I still believe that there are more of us that believe in what is common and what is decent.

So, again, thank you. Let's continue this fight for progress together.

Andrew Gillum

Paid by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor.

---

240 N. Magnolia Drive

name: Bob Hugin
subject: Thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bob Hugin for Senate, Inc
body:
| |
---
Thank you.
Although the outcome wasn’t what we wanted, this campaign has been an amazing journey. I’m proud of all of our hard work and all that we accomplished. Kathy, Hilary, Robbie, Mac and I are incredibly grateful for your support every step of the way. We put our heart and soul into this campaign. We gave it our all and will forever appreciate you standing with us. We will never forget you.
We had the great fortune of meeting so many people across all parts of our state. We ran a race where we talked about the issues that are important to all New Jerseyans ─ issues that transcend party and politics. Even though we had an election, these issues aren’t going away. For us to solve them, we’re going to have to commit to bringing people together.
Although we didn’t win, I want you to know I’m committed to fighting for a more promising future for every generation of New Jerseyans that follow. I hope all of you share that same commitment.
Lastly, we want all of you to know that we remain optimistic and plan to work together to ensure a brighter, more promising future for all New Jerseyans.
All of us on Team Hugin thank you.

Bob Hugin
|
